title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two colleagues talking about a charity event.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you helping with the charity run next month? || Woman: Yes, but I'm not running. My knee still hurts when I go far. || Man: So what will you do? || Woman: [8]I've offered to give out water near the finish line, because they need more people there[/8]. It should be busy, but at least I can still be involved. || Woman: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you helping with the charity run next month?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but I'm not running. My knee still hurts when I go far.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So what will you do?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I've offered to give out water near the finish line, because they need more people there. It should be busy, but at least I can still be involved.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

questions.question: What will the woman do at the charity event?

options.A: help runners near the end of the race
options.B: take part in the race herself

questions.explanation: She says she has offered to give out water near the finish line.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a film club.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you go to the film club again? || Woman: Yes. The film was older than I usually like, but the discussion afterwards was great. || Man: Was it very serious? || Woman: Not really. [9]People noticed things in the story that I had completely missed, so I understood the film much better[/9]. That's why I'll go again. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you go to the film club again?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. The film was older than I usually like, but the discussion afterwards was great.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Was it very serious?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. People noticed things in the story that I had completely missed, so I understood the film much better. That's why I'll go again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

questions.question: Why does the woman want to return to the film club?

options.B: The discussion helps her understand the films.
options.C: The meetings are not too serious.

questions.explanation: She says the discussion made her understand the film much better.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a weekend walk.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you still coming on the hill walk on Sunday? || Man: I want to, but I've checked the forecast. It won't rain, which is good. || Woman: So what's the problem? || Man: [10]They're expecting very strong winds at the top, and I don't enjoy walking when it's like that[/10]. Maybe we could choose a lower route. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think. || Man: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you still coming on the hill walk on Sunday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I want to, but I've checked the forecast. It won't rain, which is good.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what's the problem?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: They're expecting very strong winds at the top, and I don't enjoy walking when it's like that. Maybe we could choose a lower route.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

questions.question: Why is the man unsure about the walk?
questions.explanation: He says very strong winds are expected at the top.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking about a restaurant booking.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you manage to book the restaurant for Friday? || Man: Yes, but not the one we first wanted. It had no tables until nine thirty. || Woman: That's too late. || Man: I thought so too. [11]I booked the smaller place opposite the theatre because they could take us at seven[/11]. The menu looks simpler, but the time is better. || Man: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you manage to book the restaurant for Friday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but not the one we first wanted. It had no tables until nine thirty.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's too late.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I thought so too. I booked the smaller place opposite the theatre because they could take us at seven. The menu looks simpler, but the time is better.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

questions.question: Why did the man choose the smaller restaurant?

options.A: It had a table at a suitable time.
options.C: It was closer to his home.

questions.explanation: He says he booked the smaller place because they could take them at seven.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a new coat.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is that your new coat? It looks warm. || Woman: It is. I almost bought a black one, but everyone seems to have black coats. || Man: So you chose green? || Woman: Yes, though [12]the real reason was that this one has a hood, which I need when it rains on my walk to work[/12]. The colour was less important. || Woman: That sounds sensible. It's always easier when you know what to expect before you make a final decision.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is that your new coat? It looks warm.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is. I almost bought a black one, but everyone seems to have black coats.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So you chose green?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, though the real reason was that this one has a hood, which I need when it rains on my walk to work. The colour was less important.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible. It's always easier when you know what to expect before you make a final decision.

questions.question: Why did the woman choose the green coat?

options.A: It was cheaper than the black coat.
options.B: It has a useful feature for wet weather.
options.C: It matches most of her clothes.

questions.explanation: She says she chose it because it has a hood for rainy walks to work.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about moving to a new flat.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I've been in the new flat for two weeks now. The rooms are smaller than in my old place, but that doesn't bother me. The neighbours are quiet, and the bus stop is close. [13]What I hadn't realised was how little sunlight comes into the living room, so it feels dark even in the afternoon[/13]. I'm trying to make it brighter with lamps. || Speaker: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I've been in the new flat for two weeks now. The rooms are smaller than in my old place, but that doesn't bother me. The neighbours are quiet, and the bus stop is close. What I hadn't realised was how little sunlight comes into the living room, so it feels dark even in the afternoon. I'm trying to make it brighter with lamps.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

questions.question: What problem has the woman found with the flat?

options.B: The bus stop is too far away.
options.C: One room does not get enough light.

questions.explanation: She says the living room gets little sunlight and feels dark.
